pattern: Game theory with fixed move size: count total valid turns (each uses 1 coin of value 75 and 4 coins of value 10) and determine which player takes the last turn.
hints: Each valid turn requires exactly 1 coin worth 75 and 4 coins worth 10 (total 115).
The number of turns that can be played is min(x, y // 4).
If the total number of turns is odd, Alice (who goes first) takes the last turn and wins; if even (including 0), Bob wins.

edge_inputs.description: Exactly one turn possible — Alice wins
Not enough y coins for any turn — Bob wins immediately
Not enough x coins for any turn — Bob wins immediately
y constrains turns even though x is large
x constrains turns even though y is large
Maximum x and y — check parity of min(100, 25)
Minimum inputs — x=1, y=1, no turn possible

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 100)
    y = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [x, y]
